mechanism: Policy assignments named [Allowed|Deny|Config] + compliance area. Prefix signals effect type; compliance area is the grouping boundary. Code review is the only gate.
why: The prefix determines source-of-truth boundary: Config triggers DeployIfNotExists/Modify (Azure writes beyond Bicep); Allowed/Deny are fully represented by code. Without the prefix, enforcement posture isn't recoverable from the name.

related.note: Auth deny policies must fall under a single Deny-prefixed assignment.
Naming convention makes exemption target's enforcement posture recoverable from the record alone.
Assignment names are the navigation keys in the generated reference file — naming structure determines the IntelliSense surface for bicepparam authors.
Must use an Allowed-prefixed assignment for location compliance.
Must use a Config-prefixed assignment for diagnostic settings.
Must use a Deny-prefixed assignment for cross-tenant replication compliance.
Must use a Deny-prefixed assignment for TLS compliance.

violations: Assignment without an effect prefix (e.g. StorageNetworkRules).
Prefix outside Allowed, Deny, or Config (e.g. Audit-WeakTLS).
Display name and resource name using different prefixes.
Same compliance area split across multiple assignments.
Different compliance areas bundled under one assignment.
